title: v0.6.0 update, offline restart, and rollback verification
status: local release-candidate verification passed; hosted publication remains for issue #258

method.server: temporary localhost HTTP server with Vercel-equivalent COOP/COEP/CSP/Permissions-Policy headers
builds: pnpm --filter @speech/web build on the current candidate worktree
git worktree add --detach /tmp/speech-release-257/v0.5.0 v0.5.0 && pnpm install --frozen-lockfile && pnpm --filter @speech/web build
method.browser: Playwright Chromium with service workers enabled
notes: The same localhost origin served v0.5.0, then v0.6.0 candidate, then v0.5.0 again to exercise update and rollback service-worker replacement.
UiPreferencesV1 was written before update and remained present after update and rollback, proving the additive namespace does not delete domain data or block rollback.
No model files, audio, transcripts, vocabulary terms, profile artifacts, support bundles, or screenshots were committed.

checks.evidence: v0.5.0 app shell loaded from the tagged build on localhost and registered a controlling service worker.
With the browser context offline, the precached v0.5.0 app shell reloaded and rendered local-first speech UI text.

After switching the same origin to the v0.6 candidate build, service-worker update produced a waiting worker, SKIP_WAITING activated it, controllerchange fired, and the task-first Dictate/Vocabulary/Models shell rendered.
The namespaced speech:ui-preferences:v1 record survived the update and did not replace domain stores.
After switching the same origin back to the v0.5.0 build, a waiting service worker activated, controllerchange fired, and the v0.5 app shell rendered again without requiring data deletion.
The additive UiPreferencesV1 record remained present after rollback, demonstrating rollback-safe ignore semantics for the v0.5 app line.
apps/web/src/app/pwa-lifecycle.test.ts verifies update availability is recorded without reloading until activatePwaUpdate() is called explicitly.
apps/web/e2e/offline-model-lifecycle.spec.ts keeps the current candidate offline restart and offline indicator coverage green.

domainCompatibility.domainStores: no destructive migration was run during update or rollback verification
domainCompatibility.modelProfileVocabularyStores: existing v0.5 contracts remain unchanged by this verification issue

activeOperationUpdatePrompt.evidence: The PWA lifecycle records updateAvailable and exposes an explicit Install update action; it does not call the reload callback until activatePwaUpdate() runs. Active recording/training/import/export flows therefore are not interrupted by a passive update notice.

residualLimitations: Hosted production deployment, tag/release publication, production alias checks, and public release checksum publication belong to issue #258.
Issue #255 remains open for final moderated release-usability participant evidence or an explicit human release decision.
This evidence uses localhost same-origin release builds rather than Vercel production URLs; it is release-candidate update/rollback evidence, not a production deployment claim.
